verses.sanskrit: शौनक उवाचहत्वा स्वरिक्थस्पृध आततायिनोयुधिष्ठिरो धर्मभृतां वरिष्ठ: ।सहानुजै: प्रत्यवरुद्धभोजन:कथं प्रवृत्त: किमकारषीत्तत: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaunaka uvācahatvā svariktha-spṛdha ātatāyinoyudhiṣṭhiro dharma-bhṛtāṁ variṣṭhaḥsahānujaiḥ pratyavaruddha-bhojanaḥkathaṁ pravṛttaḥ kim akāraṣīt tataḥ
verses.text: Śaunaka Muni asked: After killing his enemies who desired to usurp his rightful inheritance, how did the greatest of all religious men, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, assisted by his brothers, rule his subjects? Surely he could not freely enjoy his kingdom with unrestricted consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचवंशं कुरोर्वंशदवाग्निनिर्हृतंसंरोहयित्वा भवभावनो हरि: ।निवेशयित्वा निजराज्य ईश्वरोयुधिष्ठिरं प्रीतमना बभूव ह ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācavaṁśaṁ kuror vaṁśa-davāgni-nirhṛtaṁsaṁrohayitvā bhava-bhāvano hariḥniveśayitvā nija-rājya īśvaroyudhiṣṭhiraṁ prīta-manā babhūva ha
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the maintainer of the world, became pleased after reestablishing Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira in his own kingdom and after restoring the Kuru dynasty, which had been exhausted by the bamboo fire of anger.

verses.sanskrit: निशम्य भीष्मोक्तमथाच्युतोक्तंप्रवृत्तविज्ञानविधूतविभ्रम: ।शशास गामिन्द्र इवाजिताश्रय:परिध्युपान्तामनुजानुवर्तित: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: niśamya bhīṣmoktam athācyutoktaṁpravṛtta-vijñāna-vidhūta-vibhramaḥśaśāsa gām indra ivājitāśrayaḥparidhyupāntām anujānuvartitaḥ
verses.text: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, after being enlightened by what was spoken by Bhīṣmadeva and Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the infallible, engaged himself in matters of perfect knowledge because all his misgivings were eradicated. Thus he ruled over the earth and seas and was followed by his younger brothers.

verses.sanskrit: कामं ववर्ष पर्जन्य: सर्वकामदुघा मही ।सिषिचु: स्म व्रजान् गाव: पयसोधस्वतीर्मुदा ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kāmaṁ vavarṣa parjanyaḥsarva-kāma-dughā mahīsiṣicuḥ sma vrajān gāvaḥpayasodhasvatīr mudā
verses.text: During the reign of Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, the clouds showered all the water that people needed, and the earth produced all the necessities of man in profusion. Due to its fatty milk bag and cheerful attitude, the cow used to moisten the grazing ground with milk.

verses.sanskrit: नद्य: समुद्रा गिरय: सवनस्पतिवीरुध: ।फलन्त्योषधय: सर्वा: काममन्वृतु तस्य वै ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: nadyaḥ samudrā girayaḥsavanaspati-vīrudhaḥphalanty oṣadhayaḥ sarvāḥkāmam anvṛtu tasya vai
verses.text: The rivers, oceans, hills, mountains, forests, creepers and active drugs, in every season, paid their tax quota to the King in profusion.

verses.sanskrit: नाधयो व्याधय: क्लेशा दैवभूतात्महेतव: ।अजातशत्रावभवन् जन्तूनां राज्ञि कर्हिचित् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: nādhayo vyādhayaḥ kleśādaiva-bhūtātma-hetavaḥajāta-śatrāv abhavanjantūnāṁ rājñi karhicit
verses.text: Because of the King’s having no enemy, the living beings were not at any time disturbed by mental agonies, diseases, or excessive heat or cold.

verses.sanskrit: उषित्वा हास्तिनपुरे मासान् कतिपयान् हरि: ।सुहृदां च विशोकाय स्वसुश्च प्रियकाम्यया ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: uṣitvā hāstinapuremāsān katipayān hariḥsuhṛdāṁ ca viśokāyasvasuś ca priya-kāmyayā
verses.text: Śrī Hari, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, resided at Hastināpura for a few months to pacify His relatives and please His own sister [Subhadrā].

verses.sanskrit: आमन्‍त्र्य चाभ्यनुज्ञात: परिष्वज्याभिवाद्य तम् ।आरुरोह रथं कैश्चित्परिष्वक्तोऽभिवादित: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: āmantrya cābhyanujñātaḥpariṣvajyābhivādya tamāruroha rathaṁ kaiścitpariṣvakto ’bhivāditaḥ
verses.text: Afterwards, when the Lord asked permission to depart and the King gave it, the Lord offered His respects to Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira by bowing down at his feet, and the King embraced Him. After this the Lord, being embraced by others and receiving their obeisances, got into His chariot.

verses.sanskrit: सुभद्रा द्रौपदी कुन्ती विराटतनया तथा ।गान्धारी धृतराष्ट्रश्च युयुत्सुर्गौतमो यमौ ॥ ९ ॥वृकोदरश्च धौम्यश्च स्त्रियो मत्स्यसुतादय: ।न सेहिरे विमुह्यन्तो विरहं शार्ङ्गधन्वन: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: subhadrā draupadī kuntīvirāṭa-tanayā tathāgāndhārī dhṛtarāṣṭraś cayuyutsur gautamo yamau
verses.text: At that time Subhadrā, Draupadī, Kuntī, Uttarā, Gāndhārī, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Yuyutsu, Kṛpācārya, Nakula, Sahadeva, Bhīmasena, Dhaumya and Satyavatī all nearly fainted because it was impossible for them to bear separation from Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सत्सङ्गान्मुक्तदु:सङ्गो हातुं नोत्सहते बुध: ।कीर्त्यमानं यशो यस्य सकृदाकर्ण्य रोचनम् ॥ ११ ॥तस्मिन्न्यस्तधिय: पार्था: सहेरन् विरहं कथम् ।दर्शनस्पर्शसंलापशयनासनभोजनै: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sat-saṅgān mukta-duḥsaṅgohātuṁ notsahate budhaḥkīrtyamānaṁ yaśo yasyasakṛd ākarṇya rocanam
verses.text: The intelligent, who have understood the Supreme Lord in association with pure devotees and have become freed from bad materialistic association, can never avoid hearing the glories of the Lord, even though they have heard them only once. How then could the Pāṇḍavas tolerate His separation, for they had been intimately associated with His person, seeing Him face to face, touching Him, conversing with Him, and sleeping, sitting and dining with Him?

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे तेऽनिमिषैरक्षैस्तमनुद्रुतचेतस: ।वीक्षन्त: स्‍नेहसम्बद्धा विचेलुस्तत्र तत्र ह ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarve te ’nimiṣair akṣaistam anu druta-cetasaḥvīkṣantaḥ sneha-sambaddhāvicelus tatra tatra ha
verses.text: All their hearts were melting for Him on the pot of attraction. They looked at Him without blinking their eyes, and they moved hither and thither in perplexity.

verses.sanskrit: न्यरुन्धन्नुद्गलद्बाष्पमौत्कण्ठ्याद्देवकीसुते ।निर्यात्यगारान्नोऽभद्रमिति स्याद्ब‍ान्धवस्त्रिय: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: nyarundhann udgalad bāṣpamautkaṇṭhyād devakī-suteniryāty agārān no ’bhadramiti syād bāndhava-striyaḥ
verses.text: The female relatives, whose eyes were flooded with tears out of anxiety for Kṛṣṇa, came out of the palace. They could stop their tears only with great difficulty. They feared that tears would cause misfortune at the time of departure.

verses.sanskrit: मृदङ्गशङ्खभेर्यश्च वीणापणवगोमुखा: ।धुन्धुर्यानकघण्टाद्या नेदुर्दुन्दुभयस्तथा ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: mṛdaṅga-śaṅkha-bheryaś cavīṇā-paṇava-gomukhāḥdhundhury-ānaka-ghaṇṭādyānedur dundubhayas tathā
verses.text: While the Lord was departing from the palace of Hastināpura, different types of drums — like the mṛdaṅga, dhola, nagra, dhundhurī and dundubhi — and flutes of different types, the vīṇā, gomukha and bherī, all sounded together to show Him honor.

verses.sanskrit: प्रासादशिखरारूढा: कुरुनार्यो दिद‍ृक्षया ।ववृषु: कुसुमै: कृष्णं प्रेमव्रीडास्मितेक्षणा: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāsāda-śikharārūḍhāḥkuru-nāryo didṛkṣayāvavṛṣuḥ kusumaiḥ kṛṣṇaṁprema-vrīḍā-smitekṣaṇāḥ
verses.text: Out of a loving desire to see the Lord, the royal ladies of the Kurus got up on top of the palace, and smiling with affection and shyness, they showered flowers upon the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: सितातपत्रं जग्राह मुक्तादामविभूषितम् ।रत्नदण्डं गुडाकेश: प्रिय: प्रियतमस्य ह ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sitātapatraṁ jagrāhamuktādāma-vibhūṣitamratna-daṇḍaṁ guḍākeśaḥpriyaḥ priyatamasya ha
verses.text: At that time Arjuna, the great warrior and conqueror of sleep, who is the intimate friend of the most beloved Supreme Lord, took up an umbrella which had a handle of jewels and was embroidered with lace and pearls.

verses.sanskrit: उद्धव: सात्यकिश्चैव व्यजने परमाद्भुते ।विकीर्यमाण: कुसुमै रेजे मधुपति: पथि ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: uddhavaḥ sātyakiś caivavyajane paramādbhutevikīryamāṇaḥ kusumaireje madhu-patiḥ pathi
verses.text: Uddhava and Sātyaki began to fan the Lord with decorated fans, and the Lord, as the master of Madhu, seated on scattered flowers, commanded them along the road.

verses.sanskrit: अश्रूयन्ताशिष: सत्यास्तत्र तत्र द्विजेरिता: ।नानुरूपानुरूपाश्च निर्गुणस्य गुणात्मन: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: aśrūyantāśiṣaḥ satyāstatra tatra dvijeritāḥnānurūpānurūpāś canirguṇasya guṇātmanaḥ
verses.text: It was being heard here and there that the benedictions being paid to Kṛṣṇa were neither befitting nor unbefitting because they were all for the Absolute, who was now playing the part of a human being.

verses.sanskrit: अन्योन्यमासीत्सञ्जल्प उत्तमश्लोकचेतसाम् ।कौरवेन्द्रपुरस्त्रीणां सर्वश्रुतिमनोहर: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: anyonyam āsīt sañjalpauttama-śloka-cetasāmkauravendra-pura-strīṇāṁsarva-śruti-mano-haraḥ
verses.text: Absorbed in the thought of the transcendental qualities of the Lord, who is sung in select poetry, the ladies on the roofs of all the houses of Hastināpura began to talk of Him. This talk was more attractive than the hymns of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: स वै किलायं पुरुष: पुरातनोय एक आसीदविशेष आत्मनि ।अग्रे गुणेभ्यो जगदात्मनीश्वरेनिमीलितात्मन्निशि सुप्तशक्तिषु ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vai kilāyaṁ puruṣaḥ purātanoya eka āsīd aviśeṣa ātmaniagre guṇebhyo jagad-ātmanīśvarenimīlitātman niśi supta-śaktiṣu
verses.text: They said: Here He is, the original Personality of Godhead as we definitely remember Him. He alone existed before the manifested creation of the modes of nature, and in Him only, because He is the Supreme Lord, all living beings merge, as if sleeping at night, their energy suspended.

verses.sanskrit: स एव भूयो निजवीर्यचोदितांस्वजीवमायां प्रकृतिं सिसृक्षतीम् ।अनामरूपात्मनि रूपनामनीविधित्समानोऽनुससार शास्त्रकृत् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa eva bhūyo nija-vīrya-coditāṁsva-jīva-māyāṁ prakṛtiṁ sisṛkṣatīmanāma-rūpātmani rūpa-nāmanīvidhitsamāno ’nusasāra śāstra-kṛt
verses.text: The Personality of Godhead, again desiring to give names and forms to His parts and parcels, the living entities, placed them under the guidance of material nature. By His own potency, material nature is empowered to re-create.

verses.sanskrit: स वा अयं यत्पदमत्र सूरयोजितेन्द्रिया निर्जितमातरिश्वन: ।पश्यन्ति भक्त्युत्कलितामलात्मनानन्वेष सत्त्वं परिमार्ष्टुमर्हति ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vā ayaṁ yat padam atra sūrayojitendriyā nirjita-mātariśvanaḥpaśyanti bhakty-utkalitāmalātmanānanv eṣa sattvaṁ parimārṣṭum arhati
verses.text: Here is the same Supreme Personality of Godhead whose transcendental form is experienced by the great devotees who are completely cleansed of material consciousness by dint of rigid devotional service and full control of life and the senses. And that is the only way to purify existence.

verses.sanskrit: स वा अयं सख्यनुगीतसत्कथोवेदेषु गुह्येषु च गुह्यवादिभि: ।य एक ईशो जगदात्मलीलयासृजत्यवत्यत्ति न तत्र सज्जते ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vā ayaṁ sakhy anugīta-sat-kathovedeṣu guhyeṣu ca guhya-vādibhiḥya eka īśo jagad-ātma-līlayāsṛjaty avaty atti na tatra sajjate
verses.text: O dear friends, here is that very Personality of Godhead whose attractive and confidential pastimes are described in the confidential parts of Vedic literature by His great devotees. It is He only who creates, maintains and annihilates the material world and yet remains unaffected.

verses.sanskrit: यदा ह्यधर्मेण तमोधियो नृपाजीवन्ति तत्रैष हि सत्त्वत: किल ।धत्ते भगं सत्यमृतं दयां यशोभवाय रूपाणि दधद्युगे युगे ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadā hy adharmeṇa tamo-dhiyo nṛpājīvanti tatraiṣa hi sattvataḥ kiladhatte bhagaṁ satyam ṛtaṁ dayāṁ yaśobhavāya rūpāṇi dadhad yuge yuge
verses.text: Whenever there are kings and administrators living like animals in the lowest modes of existence, the Lord in His transcendental form manifests His supreme power, the Truth Positive, shows special mercy to the faithful, performs wonderful activities and manifests various transcendental forms as is necessary in different periods and ages.

verses.sanskrit: अहो अलं श्लाघ्यतमं यदो: कुल-महो अलं पुण्यतमं मधोर्वनम् ।यदेष पुंसामृषभ: श्रिय: पति:स्वजन्मना चङ्‍क्रमणेन चाञ्चति ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho alaṁ ślāghyatamaṁ yadoḥ kulamaho alaṁ puṇyatamaṁ madhor vanamyad eṣa puṁsām ṛṣabhaḥ śriyaḥ patiḥsva-janmanā caṅkramaṇena cāñcati
verses.text: Oh, how supremely glorified is the dynasty of King Yadu, and how virtuous is the land of Mathurā, where the supreme leader of all living beings, the husband of the goddess of fortune, has taken His birth and wandered in His childhood.

verses.sanskrit: अहो बत स्वर्यशसस्तिरस्करीकुशस्थली पुण्ययशस्करी भुव: ।पश्यन्ति नित्यं यदनुग्रहेषितंस्मितावलोकं स्वपतिं स्म यत्प्रजा: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho bata svar-yaśasas tiraskarīkuśasthalī puṇya-yaśaskarī bhuvaḥpaśyanti nityaṁ yad anugraheṣitaṁsmitāvalokaṁ sva-patiṁ sma yat-prajāḥ
verses.text: Undoubtedly it is wonderful that Dvārakā has defeated the glories of the heavenly planets and has enhanced the celebrity of the earth. The inhabitants of Dvārakā are always seeing the soul of all living beings [Kṛṣṇa] in His loving feature. He glances at them and favors them with sweet smiles.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं व्रतस्‍नानहुतादिनेश्वर:समर्चितो ह्यस्य गृहीतपाणिभि: ।पिबन्ति या: सख्यधरामृतं मुहु-र्व्रजस्त्रिय: सम्मुमुहुर्यदाशया: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ vrata-snāna-hutādineśvaraḥsamarcito hy asya gṛhīta-pāṇibhiḥpibanti yāḥ sakhy adharāmṛtaṁ muhurvraja-striyaḥ sammumuhur yad-āśayāḥ
verses.text: O friends, just think of His wives, whose hands He has accepted. How they must have undergone vows, baths, fire sacrifices and perfect worship of the Lord of the universe to constantly relish now the nectar from His lips [by kissing]. The damsels of Vrajabhūmi would often faint just by expecting such favors.

verses.sanskrit: या वीर्यशुल्केन हृता: स्वयंवरेप्रमथ्य चैद्यप्रमुखान् हि शुष्मिण: ।प्रद्युम्नसाम्बाम्बसुतादयोऽपरायाश्चाहृता भौमवधे सहस्रश: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yā vīrya-śulkena hṛtāḥ svayaṁvarepramathya caidya-pramukhān hi śuṣmiṇaḥpradyumna-sāmbāmba-sutādayo ’parāyāś cāhṛtā bhauma-vadhe sahasraśaḥ
verses.text: The children of these ladies are Pradyumna, Sāmba, Amba, etc. Ladies like Rukmiṇī, Satyabhāmā and Jāmbavatī were forcibly taken away by Him from their svayaṁvara ceremonies after He defeated many powerful kings, headed by Śiśupāla. And other ladies were also forcibly taken away by Him after He killed Bhaumāsura and thousands of his assistants. All of these ladies are glorious.

verses.sanskrit: एता: परं स्त्रीत्वमपास्तपेशलंनिरस्तशौचं बत साधु कुर्वते ।यासां गृहात्पुष्करलोचन: पति-र्न जात्वपैत्याहृतिभिर्हृदि स्पृशन् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: etāḥ paraṁ strītvam apāstapeśalaṁnirasta-śaucaṁ bata sādhu kurvateyāsāṁ gṛhāt puṣkara-locanaḥ patirna jātv apaity āhṛtibhir hṛdi spṛśan
verses.text: All these women auspiciously glorified their lives despite their being without individuality and without purity. Their husband, the lotus-eyed Personality of Godhead, never left them alone at home. He always pleased their hearts by making valuable presentations.

verses.sanskrit: एवंविधा गदन्तीनां स गिर: पुरयोषिताम् ।निरीक्षणेनाभिनन्दन् सस्मितेन ययौ हरि: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁvidhā gadantīnāṁsa giraḥ pura-yoṣitāmnirīkṣaṇenābhinandansasmitena yayau hariḥ
verses.text: While the ladies of the capital [Hastināpura] were greeting Him and talking in this way, the Lord, smiling, accepted their good greetings, and casting the grace of His glance over them, He departed from the city.

verses.sanskrit: अजातशत्रु: पृतनां गोपीथाय मधुद्विष: ।परेभ्य: शङ्कित: स्‍नेहात्प्रायुङ्क्त चतुरङ्गिणीम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ajāta-śatruḥ pṛtanāṁgopīthāya madhu-dviṣaḥparebhyaḥ śaṅkitaḥ snehātprāyuṅkta catur-aṅgiṇīm
verses.text: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, although no one’s enemy, engaged four divisions of defense [horse, elephant, chariot and army] to accompany Lord Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of the asuras [demons]. The Mahārāja did this because of the enemy, and also out of affection for the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: अथ दूरागतान् शौरि: कौरवान् विरहातुरान् ।सन्निवर्त्य द‍ृढं स्‍निग्धान् प्रायात्स्वनगरीं प्रियै: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha dūrāgatān śauriḥkauravān virahāturānsannivartya dṛḍhaṁ snigdhānprāyāt sva-nagarīṁ priyaiḥ
verses.text: Out of profound affection for Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Pāṇḍavas, who were of the Kuru dynasty, accompanied Him a considerable distance to see Him off. They were overwhelmed with the thought of future separation. The Lord, however, persuaded them to return home, and He proceeded towards Dvārakā with His dear companions.

verses.sanskrit: कुरुजाङ्गलपाञ्चालान् शूरसेनान् सयामुनान् ।ब्रह्मावर्तं कुरुक्षेत्रं मत्स्यान् सारस्वतानथ ॥ ३४ ॥मरुधन्वमतिक्रम्य सौवीराभीरयो: परान् ।आनर्तान् भार्गवोपागाच्छ्रान्तवाहो मनाग्विभु: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kuru-jāṅgala-pāñcālānśūrasenān sayāmunānbrahmāvartaṁ kurukṣetraṁmatsyān sārasvatān atha
verses.text: O Śaunaka, the Lord then proceeded towards Kurujāṅgala, Pāñcālā, Śūrasenā, the land on the bank of the river Yamunā, Brahmāvarta, Kurukṣetra, Matsya, Sārasvata, the province of the desert and the land of scanty water. After crossing these provinces He gradually reached the Sauvīra and Ābhīra provinces, then, west of these, reached Dvārakā at last.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र तत्र ह तत्रत्यैर्हरि: प्रत्युद्यतार्हण: ।सायं भेजे दिशं पश्चाद्गविष्ठो गां गतस्तदा ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra tatra ha tatratyairhariḥ pratyudyatārhaṇaḥsāyaṁ bheje diśaṁ paścādgaviṣṭho gāṁ gatas tadā
verses.text: On His journey through these provinces He was welcomed, worshiped and given various presentations. In the evening, in all places, the Lord suspended His journey to perform evening rites. This was regularly observed after sunset.
